name: Hoppin' John
recipeInstructions:
1. If using dried black-eyed peas, reserve the stock after cooking them.
2. In a large skillet, sauté onions and garlic in vegetable oil until golden, about 10 minutes.
3. Add allspice, cayenne, and tamari (if using) to the skillet. Stir this mixture into the cooked peas with enough reserved stock to make it juicy.
4. Simmer gently for about 20 minutes, stirring often, to allow flavors to marry.
5. To serve, spoon the black-eyed peas over cooked rice and top with chopped tomatoes, scallions, and parsley.
